title: Dzoraget Gorge Loop
excerpt: A dramatic canyon walk along rust-red tuff cliffs above the Dzoraget River, passing ancient cave hermitages carved directly into the rock.

title: Haghpat Monastery
excerpt: UNESCO-listed 10th-century monastery complex rising from a forested plateau above the Debed Canyon. Armenia's finest example of medieval sacred architecture.

title: Akner Village
excerpt: Perched at 1,400 m between forest ridges, Akner is one of Lori's most intact historic villages — stone houses, walnut groves, and a 13th-century church.

title: Lori Berd Fortress
excerpt: 11th-century fortress city on a dramatic cliff peninsula — the capital of the Kingdom of Lori, abandoned 900 years ago and never rebuilt.

title: Sanahin Monastery
excerpt: Twin to Haghpat and equally remarkable — a sprawling 10th-century complex with the birthplace of the legendary Mikoyan brothers beside it.

title: Debed Canyon Traverse
excerpt: Follow the Debed River north from Alaverdi through copper-stained gorge walls, Soviet-era bridges, and dense forest — one of Lori's most varied walks.

title: Dsegh Village
excerpt: The birthplace of the poet Hovhannes Tumanyan, set in a gentle valley with a charming museum-house and surrounding meadows perfect for a slow afternoon.

title: Alaverdi Copper Culture
excerpt: Meet the last craftsmen working Lori's 3,000-year-old copper tradition — a guided half-day with a local metalsmith, foundry visit, and hands-on workshop.

title: Lori Carpet Weaving
excerpt: Learn the indigo-and-vordan-karmir dyeing process that's made Lori carpets distinct for centuries — a small-group workshop with a master weaver in Stepanavan.

title: Jijur Ridge Walk
excerpt: A high alpine traverse above 2,000 m with panoramic views across both the Debed and Pambak valleys — best in late summer when the meadows are in full bloom.

title: Kobayr Monastery
excerpt: Half-ruined, half-magnificent — a 12th-century cliff-side monastery with remarkable Byzantine frescoes still clinging to crumbling walls above the Debed.

title: Tashir Lake
excerpt: A hidden alpine lake in the north of Lori, rarely visited and surrounded by wild meadows — bring a tent and stay the night for the full effect.

title: Akhtala Monastery
excerpt: A 10th-century fortress-monastery with the most complete surviving Byzantine frescoes in the South Caucasus. Largely unvisited, spectacularly intact.

title: Odzun Basilica
excerpt: One of Armenia's oldest churches — a 6th-century basilica on a high plateau above the Debed, with sweeping views and almost no other visitors.

title: Sanahin Bridge
excerpt: A single-arch medieval bridge spanning the Debed gorge ravine — 800 years old, still sound, and still used as a footpath between two villages.

title: Odzun Village
excerpt: Perched on a wide plateau 1,100 m above the Debed canyon, Odzun looks like the edge of something — fields, then forest, then a 400 m drop.

title: Stepanavan Dendropark
excerpt: A 35-hectare park planted in 1933 with 620 species of trees from five continents. Come in autumn for the colour, or spring for the understory wildflowers.

title: Mets Mantash
excerpt: One of Lori's largest traditional villages, spread across a hillside south of Stepanavan. Stone barns, khachkar-filled churchyards, and a weekly market.

title: Wild Herb Foraging Walk
excerpt: A guided half-day walk through Lori's forest edge, learning to identify, harvest and cook with 15 edible plants that grow in the region from May to September.

title: Honey Day in Akner
excerpt: Visit Vardan Hakobyan's forty hives at the forest edge above Akner — a morning among the hives, tasting linden, chestnut, and wildflower honeys from the jar.

title: Pambak Ridge Traverse
excerpt: A demanding high-alpine crossing along the Pambak range at 2,400 m — panoramic views north to Georgia, south to Aragats, and nothing but cloud and stone between.

title: Alaverdi Soviet Gorge Walk
excerpt: The strangest town in Armenia: a copper-smelting city built vertically into a 350 m gorge, connected by a Soviet cable car still running since 1958.
